sections.name: Subject Line (Permission Frame)
sections.content:
This our last email, and that's okay

> Character count: 40 /* Under 50, within S2 spec */ 
> Hook type: Permission frame (per S2 Subject Line Matrix §6, Sunset E2 maps to Permission frame category) 
> Cross-level check: No age, no label, no diagnosis language. Universal to all sub-groups (A–E).

---

## Preview Text

You're always welcome back. But if today isn't the day, that's a real answer.

> Character count: 92 
> Cross-level check: Zero-pressure, no guilt. Respects every emotional phase from Phase 1 (still hopeful) through Phase 4 (resigned). Uses universal closer language per Layer-17 §10.

---

## From Name

Lena from BrightKidCo

> > Reply-To:** support@brightkidco.com

---

sections.name: Hook / Observation
sections.content:
This our last email.

We haven't heard from you in a while, and we're respecting your inbox. If today isn't the right time, that's a real answer. The product will be here when you're ready. So will I.

> > Cross-level calibration (R1–R6):** 
> R1, No level labels. No diagnosis language. 
> R2, Recognition anchors implied: "we haven't heard from you" acknowledges the parent has been unengaged (could be L1 busy with Pre-K prep, L2 deep in therapy cycles, L3 resigned, or Sub-Group B/C/D/E). 
> R3, Age ranges: "when you're ready" is age-neutral. 
> R4, Verbal/non-verbal inclusive: "respecting your inbox" is format-neutral, no child action assumed. 
> R5, Timeline flexibility: "when you're ready" is open-ended. 
> R6, Low bar success: "if today isn't the right time" redefines success as self-awareness, not product purchase. 
> Voice rule V1 (Peer-to-peer): "we're respecting your inbox", sounds like a person, not a brand. 
> Voice rule V4 (Never sound like marketing): No retargeting language, no "we miss you," no urgency.

---

sections.name: Recognition Anchors (R2 Block)
sections.content:
Whether your child talks about the potty but doesn't use it, or you've been at this for years and stopped counting the attempts, if the signal isn't arriving, you're not alone in that.

Some kids are 3 and just starting to show signs. Others are 10+ and you've tried everything. Some parents have just begun looking. Others have stepped back from trying entirely. Every version belongs here.

> > Cross-level calibration (R1–R6):** 
> R1, Symptom-first: "talks about the potty but doesn't use it" (L1 anchor), "years and stopped counting" (L3 anchor). 
> R2, Four recognition anchors: (1) talks but doesn't use, (2) years of attempts, (3) age 3 vs 10+, (4) just looking vs stepped back. 
> R3, Age range: "3" to "10+" explicitly. 
> R4, Verbal/non-verbal inclusive: "talks about the potty" (verbal) alongside "stopped counting" (action-based, no speech assumed). 
> R5, Timeline flexibility: "just beginning" and "years" cover the full spectrum. 
> R6, "You're not alone" = low-bar validation, no promise of outcome. 
> Which sub-groups addressed: 
> Sub-Group A: "just starting to show signs" 
> Sub-Group B: "if the signal isn't arriving", symptom language, no label 
> Sub-Group C: "nervous system processes signals differently" via mechanism section 
> Sub-Group D: "tried everything", validation for the overwhelmed 
> Sub-Group E: "you're not alone", generic enough for cart abandoners with no context

---

sections.name: Validation + Mechanism (Gentle)
sections.content:
If you've stepped back from trying, I get it. Both my sons went through this. My older son was 8 before we figured out what was really going on. And I know what it feels like when every method you try ends the same way.

What we've learned, from the research and from thousands of families, is that it's not about effort. It's not about motivation. For kids whose nervous systems process body signals differently, the standard methods were solving the wrong problem.

The signal that says "I need to go", interoception, arrives differently for some kids. It arrives too late, or it doesn't arrive at all. And no method that assumes it's already there can bridge that gap.

> > Voice rules: V1 (Peer-to-peer):** "Both my sons went through this", Lena's background surfacing. 
> V7 (Validate before solving): First validates stepping back, then offers mechanism. 
> V10 (Autism is identity, not disease): "nervous systems process body signals differently", not "broken" or "wrong." 
> V11 (Mechanism specificity): Explicitly names interoception, explains how the signal arrives too late. 
> V13 (Science-first): References "research from thousands of families." 
> Cross-level calibration (R1–R6): 
> R1, "kids whose nervous systems process body signals differently", symptom language without labels. 
> R3, No fixed age mentioned (the 8-year reference is Lena's personal story, not a child-age prescription). 
> R4, "doesn't arrive at all" covers non-verbal children. 
> R5, No timeline promised whatsoever. 
> R6, Validation as win: "you stepped back" is framed as a legitimate choice, not failure.

---

### SECTION 4: Permission / Gentle Close

If your child's situation has changed, if you're ready to try something different down the road, you're always welcome back. If today isn't the day, that's okay. The science will still be here. The Body-Signal Learning Layer will still be here. So will I.

Whichever path you take, thanks for letting us into your inbox. You've been doing the work. That's what matters.

> > Voice rules:** V12 (Permission-not-to-buy creates trust), explicitly gives permission to leave. 
> V15 (Lena sign-off is universal trust lever). 
> V4 (Never sound like marketing), "thanks for letting us into your inbox" instead of "thank you for your interest." 
> Cross-level calibration (R1–R6): 
> R6, "You've been doing the work" = success defined as showing up, not as purchasing or training. 
> No CTA, per S2 §1.22 structure: "E2: Goodbye → Lena (LONG) → NO CTA."

---

## Lena Sign-Off (LONG, Final Goodbye Variant)

With care,

Lena,
Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons

P.S. My older son was 8 before we figured this out. If you've stepped back from trying, I get it. When you're ready, the science will still be here. 60 days, your judgment. [https://brightkidco.com]

> > Why LONG variant on E2:** S2 explicitly specifies LONG for Sunset E2 as the final email. Per 11-brand-voice-reference.md §9.2, emails 6+ / Winback / Phase 4 use "With care" closing. Sunset E2 is the absolute final touchpoint, aligns with deepest sign-off variant. 
> Voice rule V3: Profile subtitle present. 
> Voice rule V15: Lena sign-off = universal trust lever across ALL levels. 
> Cross-level check: P.S. content is universal, no level specificity, no age assumptions, no promise of outcomes. 60-day guarantee referenced as standing offer, not as push.

---
